[Editor] Test Assessment Generation
This notebook runs the BridgeAI assessment generator so you can try different job descriptions and see:
1. Step 1 output — Requirements extraction: inferred stack, level, confidence, summary, and key skills from the job description.
2. Final assessment — The generated title, description, and time limit as candidates will see it.
3. Review feedback (if any) — After generation, a rule-based check and an LLM quality/feasibility review run. If issues remain after one retry, reviewFeedback is shown so you can see what failed (e.g. word count, sections, or feasibility).
Pipeline: LCEL chain (Step 1 -> routing -> Step 2 -> normalize) then rule-based review + LLM review; failed reviews trigger one retry with feedback before returning.
Setup:
1. Install Jupyter: pip install jupyter (if needed).
2. From the repo root (bridge-assessments/): run jupyter notebook or jupyter lab, then open notebooks/test-assessment-generation.ipynb.
3. Ensure server/config.env exists and has your API key (OPENAI_API_KEY or Anthropic/Gemini).
When running the notebook from repo root, set env first if needed: export $(grep -v '^#' server/config.env | xargs) (or run from server/ with config.env loaded).
4. Edit the job description in the next cell and run the cells (Run All, or run cell by cell).
Note: The notebook runs the same TypeScript script the app uses (server/src/scripts/test-assessment-generation.ts) with --steps so you get both Step 1 and the final assessment. No server or database required.

read through th whole codebase....
fences that GPT sometimes wraps around.
Bug fix — manifestInjector.ts:8-14: Same issue — Transcriptline interface required description as non-optional. Now accepts both fields.
Debug logs added to:
generator.ts — logs at every step: frame count, batch count, raw API output (first 500 chars), stitched line count, final line count, and full error + stack on failure
visionClients.ts — logs model name, image count, response length, finish_reason, token counts, and warns on empty responses
stitcherts.ts — logs parsed segment count per batch, skipped lines, and total segments

All logs are prefixed with [transcript], [vision], or [stitchert] so you can easily find them in the server console. Try generating again — the server terminal should show exactly where it’s failing or what the API is returning.
